assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has Docker installed if self-hosting
Developer has basic knowledge of web or mobile app development

developer_goal: Create an Appwrite account and project to build a backend for a web or mobile application.

first_success.outcome: Developer has a backend project with a database collection ready to be used by their app
first_success.observable_signal: New project and database collection appear in the Appwrite console UI

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Self-hosted Appwrite server setup
candidates.reason: Present in the machine draft; not selected by an independent shortest-path audit.

candidates.name: Self-hosted CLI or direct API calls
candidates.reason: While possible, the primary documented onboarding flow uses the web console and SDKs rather than CLI or raw API calls.

required_path.action: Sign up for an Appwrite account or log in

required_fields.label: Email, password, organization name
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Create a new project in the Appwrite console

required_fields.label: Project name, region
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.observable_result: Project dashboard loads with project details

required_path.action: Create a new database within the project

required_fields.label: Database name and optional ID
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Create a collection (table) in the database

required_fields.label: Collection name and optional ID
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_fields.label: Attribute name, type, and constraints
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Create a document in the collection

required_fields.label: Document field values
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Add a platform for the app (web, mobile, native)

required_fields.label: Platform type and identifier (domain, package ID)
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Initialize Appwrite SDK in app code with endpoint and project ID

required_fields.label: Endpoint URL, project ID
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Make first API call to fetch or create data using SDK

required_fields.label: API call parameters
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

prerequisites.description: Create an Appwrite account via the Appwrite Cloud or self-host an Appwrite server
Basic familiarity with web or mobile app development and SDK integration

external_gates.description: Creating an Appwrite account is required to access the cloud console and create projects.
Docker installation is required for self-hosting Appwrite locally.
SDK initialization requires correct endpoint URL and project ID configuration.
API calls require valid credentials or API keys depending on client type (client or admin).

platform_outcomes.description: Create a new project in the Appwrite console and configure a database with a collection

uncertainties.question: Is there an officially documented single 'first success' milestone explicitly named by Appwrite?
uncertainties.impact: The boundary of first success is inferred from multiple steps rather than a single explicit milestone, which may affect precision.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: No explicit single milestone labeled as 'first success' was found in the official docs; the milestone was synthesized from quickstart and tutorial steps.

uncertainties.question: Exact time to first success is not explicitly stated by Appwrite.
uncertainties.impact: Time estimate is approximate based on tutorial narrative and may vary.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: No explicit timing claim found in official docs; estimate derived from tutorial video narration.

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Appwrite route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
